highest_priority: hand position relative to work area
notes: Stage 2 should emphasize hand and work-area interaction more than full-body pose.
Idle means present without productive hand-work interaction.
Get and put are transitional hand actions and should be reviewed carefully.
Use Gemini for structured segment labeling with local validation, not as unconstrained final truth.
Gemini outputs must include role-aware cycle phase fields and pass repo-side consistency checks before cycle counting.
Gemini video understanding samples video at 1 FPS by default, so rapid micro-actions may still need local temporal logic.

training_plan.phase_a: train on labeled activity seed images
training_plan.phase_c: fine-tune on reviewed video-domain ROI crops
training_plan.phase_d: temporal smoothing and cycle counting at inference

notes: Start with image classification because the current activity dataset is still small.
Do not rely on Gemini or pseudo-labels as final activity supervision without review.
Upgrade to sequence modeling only after enough clip-level labels exist.
Cycle counting should use a state machine over activity predictions, not raw frame labels alone.
Hand/work-area evidence should dominate Stage 2 decisions whenever available.
